LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEFENDANT COVE CONSTRUCTION, MICHAEL TEAGUE, ROBERT CAIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST M.C. PLASTERING CO., INC. ON THE DUTY TO DEFEND, THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED IN PART FOR REASONS STATED ON THE RECORD. MC PLASTERING HAS A DUTY TO DEFEND, BUT IT APPEARS SO DO OTHER SUBS. THEREFORE, WE ARE IN THE PRECISE SETTING THAT THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT ANTICIPATED FOOTNOTE 12 AND THE COURT EXERCISES IT'S DISCRETION TO WAIT OR DEFER A DETERMINTATION AS TO THE IMMEDIATE REQUIREMENT FOR MC PLASTERING TO DEFEND. MR. BORBELY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. Off Calendar Jun-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Brumfield, Esq. 510-834-4350 of Boornazian, Jensen & Garthe for Defendant COVE CONSTRUCTION, MICHAEL TEAGUE, ROBERT CAIN.
Thomas E. Borbely, Esq. 415-785-4547 of the Ellis Law Group for Cross-Defendant Lawson Roofing.
Jonathan Kepko, Esq. 415-362-2580 of Lewis Brisbois for Cross-Defendant MC Plastering.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT COVE CONSTRUCTION, MICHAEL TEAGUE, ROBERT CAIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against M.C. Plastering Co., Inc. On The Duty To Defend.

Granted in part for reasons stated on the record. MC plastering has a duty to defend, but it appears so do other subs. Therefore, we are in the precise setting that the California Supreme Court anticipated footnote 12 and the Court exercises it's discretion to wait or defer a determintation as to the immediate requirement for MC plastering to defend.

Mr. Borbely to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against M.C. Plastering Co., Inc. On The Duty To Defend
Off Calendar Jun-07-2018 Continued To Jun-14-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Lauren A. Pagan of Boornazian jensen & Garthe, Tel. No. 1-510-834-4350 appearing for the Defendant and Cross-Complainant;
Atty. Jonathan G. Kepko of Lewis Brisbois, Tel. No. 1-415-438-6646 appearing for the
Defendants and Cross-Defendant.

RULING - The matter was discussed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross-Complainants Cove Construction, Michael Teague, and Robert Cain's motion for summary adjudication against M.C. Plastering Co., Inc. on the issue of duty to defend is continued to June 14, 2018 to give the moving parties and all whom they contend owe them a duty to defend to work out an agreement "how such a defense should be provided or financed." (Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 565, n.12.) . Per the indemnification provision of its contract with Cove and Civil Code 2778(4), M.C. has an ongoing duty to defend Cove since plaintiffs Nanci Clarence and Lidia Szajko have alleged that M.C.'s negligent stucco work caused in part the defects they are complaining about. (Crawford). However, because there appears to be several other subcontractors in a similar position to M.C. with regard to a duty to defend owed to Cove, footnote 12 in Crawford teaches that this court, before making a ruling on the timing and scope of M.C.'s duty to defend, should give the "affected parties" an opportunity to agree on "how such a defense should be provided or financed." As explained in footnote 12, when "an ongoing duty to defend is owed by multiple parties" under their respective indemnification agreements, the court has discretion to "permit the litigation to proceed with counsel chosen and paid by the party to whom the duty is owed, subject to a later determination of how damages for breach of the duty to defend should be apportioned among the breaching parties," but this discretion should only be exercised after the "parties, acting in good faith'cannot agree on how such a defense should be provided or financed." (Crawford, n. 12.) Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk
